title: $1M MSCRF grant powers novel adipose allograft therapy for osteoarthritis
excerpt: $1M grant from the Maryland Stem Cell Research Fund (MSCRF) powers novel adipose tissue allograft therapy for osteoarthritis.
body: Britecyte announced a $1,000,000 Clinical Program Grant from the Maryland Stem Cell Research Fund (MSCRF) supporting novel adipose tissue allograft therapy for osteoarthritis.

The award advances Britecyte’s clinical program evaluating BRC-OA, a human adipose allograft, for the treatment of knee osteoarthritis — a serious and debilitating disease affecting tens of millions of adults.

title: MSCRF awards $1M clinical grant for BRC-OA knee osteoarthritis study
excerpt: Britecyte’s Phase 1/2a multicenter study of BRC-OA, a human adipose allograft for knee osteoarthritis, was awarded a $1,000,000 Clinical Program Grant from MSCRF.
body: Britecyte’s clinical study — “A Phase 1/2a, Multicenter, Randomized, Controlled, Single-Blind, Dose-Finding Study to Evaluate the Safety and Efficacy of BRC-OA, a Human Adipose Allograft, for Treatment of Knee Osteoarthritis” — was awarded a $1,000,000 Clinical Program Grant from the Maryland Stem Cell Research Fund (MSCRF).
The study is designed to evaluate safety and dose-finding for BRC-OA in patients with knee osteoarthritis.

title: Britecyte’s adipose tissue allograft earns FDA “Safe to Proceed” status
excerpt: FDA “safe to proceed” status for BRC-OA marks a significant step toward clinical evaluation in osteoarthritis.
body: Amarex announced that work on behalf of Britecyte resulted in a “safe to proceed” status from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for Britecyte’s Adipose Tissue Allograft (BRC-OA).
BRC-OA is intended for the treatment of osteoarthritis (OA). OA affects over 30 million people and is the most common cause of disability in adults.

“Regenerative medicine products may provide renewed hope for patients to walk without pain,” said Alla Danilkovitch, Founder and Chief Scientific Officer of Britecyte. “Our collaboration with Amarex has resulted in the successful initiation of the clinical phase for our development program.”

Potential BRC-OA benefits highlighted in the announcement include on-demand availability, prolonged storage, minimally invasive injection, no matching or immunosuppression required, and a robust manufacturing and testing process.

title: LifeLink Tissue Bank and Britecyte join forces on adipose tissue technology
excerpt: LifeLink® Tissue Bank and Britecyte® announce a strategic collaboration on recovery, processing, and clinical application of adipose tissue.
body: LifeLink Tissue Bank, a non-profit leader in tissue allografts, announced a strategic relationship with Britecyte to advance recovery, processing, and clinical application of adipose tissue.

“We are proud to align ourselves with LifeLink Tissue Bank in this groundbreaking venture,” said Samson Tom, President, CEO, and Co-Founder of Britecyte. “The partnership leverages the strengths of both organizations to advance the field of regenerative medicine, particularly in the clinical use of adipose tissue.”

The collaboration supports a platform that provides an alternative to autologous fat grafting for soft tissue repair, including challenges associated with fat pad atrophy. The partnership was highlighted with the introduction of Liposana™ at the American College of Foot and Ankle Surgeons (ACFAS) meeting in Tampa in February 2024.

title: MSCRF awards $270K commercialization grant for NASH research
excerpt: Britecyte’s research project on a mesenchymal stem cell–containing matrix for NASH was awarded a $270,000 Commercialization Program Grant from MSCRF.
body: Britecyte’s research project “A Mesenchymal Stem Cell Containing Matrix for Nonalcoholic Steatohepatitis (NASH)” was awarded a $270,000 Commercialization Program Grant from the Maryland Stem Cell Research Fund (MSCRF).
The award supported early commercialization work advancing Britecyte’s adipose-based regenerative medicine platform.
